examples.title: AI research report acceptance
examples.input: A public-safe summary of an AI research deliverable and its evidence categories.
examples.process: GoalOS maps claims to criteria, flags gaps, preserves limitations, and prepares a human-review-ready decision state.
examples.output: Evidence Docket, verifier report, risk ledger, executive brief, decision deck, action graph, Chronicle entry, capability package, and Mission Receipt.
examples.decision: Accepted with visible limitations and one follow-up question.

examples.title: Automation delivery review
examples.input: A public-safe milestone description, acceptance checklist, and proof categories.
examples.process: GoalOS checks the delivery against criteria, test evidence, runbook completeness, known limitations, and rollback path.
examples.output: Verifier report, risk ledger, action graph, acceptance decision state, and signed Mission Receipt.
examples.decision: Ready for human approval after final runbook check.

examples.title: Enterprise AI adoption decision
examples.input: A non-sensitive summary of an AI adoption proposal and its supporting materials.
examples.process: GoalOS separates supported claims from assumptions, risk items, and reviewer decisions.
examples.output: Executive architecture brief, claim matrix, source provenance, risk ledger, and decision deck.
examples.decision: Request changes until security and governance evidence are complete.

examples.title: Market-entry proof mission
examples.input: A public-safe market thesis and evidence categories.
examples.process: GoalOS checks competitor coverage, source age, contradictions, assumptions, and decision readiness.
examples.output: Evidence Docket, market-risk ledger, decision state, action graph, and receipt.
examples.decision: Ready for leadership review with open risk notes.

examples.title: Grant / milestone verification
examples.input: Public-safe milestone summary, deliverable list, and proof pointers.
examples.process: GoalOS maps promised outputs to evidence, reviewer notes, open items, and accepted version.
examples.output: Milestone Evidence Docket, reviewer report, decision state, and signed receipt.
examples.decision: Accepted for the specified version only.

examples.title: Procurement or vendor review
examples.input: Public-safe vendor claim list and evidence categories.
examples.process: GoalOS maps claims to evidence, flags missing security material, and structures the adoption decision.
examples.output: Claims matrix, risk ledger, source provenance, reviewer notes, and decision deck.

examples.title: Defensive cybersecurity readiness review
examples.input: Public-safe defensive readiness summary with no secrets, credentials, exploit code, or target data.
examples.process: GoalOS checks control evidence, safety boundaries, review path, and remediation readiness.
examples.output: Readiness Evidence Docket, safety ledger, action graph, and decision state.
examples.decision: Escalate to qualified security review before operational use.

pricing.name: Private Beta
pricing.description: Early proof missions and product feedback.
pricing.bestFor: Trusted early users and public-safe examples.

pricing.name: Proof Mission
pricing.description: One AI-delivered work package turned into an Evidence Docket and decision state.
pricing.bestFor: Consultants, agencies, founders, grant programs, and AI teams.

pricing.name: Team / Institution
pricing.description: Repeatable proof missions, templates, receipt workflows, and team review posture.
pricing.bestFor: Organizations that need recurring acceptance records.

glossary.Mission: The work package and decision that need acceptance.
glossary.Evidence Docket: A public-safe proof room connecting claims, evidence, risks, verifier notes, and replay path.
glossary.Verifier report: A structured review of claim support, missing evidence, contradictions, risk, and readiness.
glossary.Mission Receipt: A signed record of the accepted version, decision state, evidence hashes, issuer, and timestamp.
glossary.Claim boundary: A clear line between what is supported, uncertain, not claimed, or reserved for human review.
glossary.Proof mission: A focused process that turns AI-delivered work into an evidence-backed decision package.
glossary.Governed decision state: The inspectable state that lets a reviewer accept, request changes, reject, or escalate.
glossary.Chronicle: A durable record of mission, proof, decision, and reusable capability.
glossary.Capability package: Reusable templates, criteria, evidence patterns, risk lessons, and review structure from a completed mission.
